subject: Statistics
title: Why We Need Clinical Trials
description: A 60-second lesson on Why We Need Clinical Trials.

hook.text: For centuries, doctors believed bloodletting cured disease. It took controlled trials to prove them wrong.

buildup.text: Without trials, doctors relied on intuition and anecdotes. Harmful treatments persisted for generations.

discovery.text: Clinical trials compare a treatment group to a control group. The difference reveals the drug's true effect.

twist.text: Many 'obvious' treatments failed trials. Bed rest for back pain, antibiotics for ear infections — both debunked.

climax.text: Modern medicine is built on clinical trials. Without them, we'd still be guessing what works.

punchline.text: Test it or guess. Medicine chose testing.

quiz.question: Why are clinical trials essential in medicine?
options: They separate effective treatments from harmful or useless ones through controlled comparison
They make drugs cheaper to produce
They eliminate all side effects from treatments
